message.content:
In /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbw/rbupmis_Scrub.sh, transform 2 instances of `&& continue` to inverted-test `|| continue`:

- Line 52: `[[ "$clean_line" == "Looking up Podman Machine image at"* ]] && continue` → `[[ "$clean_line" != "Looking up Podman Machine image at"* ]] || continue`
- Line 53: `[[ "$clean_line" == *"podman machine start"* ]] && continue` → `[[ "$clean_line" != *"podman machine start"* ]] || continue`

Read the file first, then make both edits. Do not change anything else.

content.content:
1→#!/bin/bash
     2→# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
     3→#
     4→# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5→# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6→# You may obtain a copy of the License at
     7→#
     8→#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9→#
    10→# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11→# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12→# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13→# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14→# limitations under the License.
    15→#
    16→# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
    17→#
    18→
    19→# Podman Machine Init Scrubber
    20→#
    21→#  This project builds confidence that the default podman VM image
    22→#  retrieved by podman is the same as the one selected by this project
    23→#  as determined from other means.  This script here removes the
    24→#  accidental aspects so that we can compare the big sha that is
    25→#  displayed to tag the VM version in use precisely.
    26→
    27→# Reference stdout from podman machine init, below:
    28→#
    29→# Looking up Podman Machine image at quay.io/podman/machine-os-wsl:5.5 to create VM
    30→# 35e8036263febe8a2a7250dc83581a85f1771e9e49c4ccddfc0d7989473802fc
    31→# Importing operating system into WSL (this may take a few minutes on a new WSL install)...
    32→# The operation completed successfully.
    33→# Configuring system...
    34→# Machine init complete
    35→# To start your machine run:
    36→#
    37→#         podman machine start rbw-example
    38→#
    39→
    40→set -euo pipefail
    41→
    42→outfile="$1"
    43→
    44→# Ensure output file is empty
    45→> "$outfile"
    46→
    47→while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
    48→  # Strip nulls from line
    49→  clean_line="${line//$'\x00'/}"
    50→
    51→  # Skip lines matching...
    52→  [[ "$clean_line" == "Looking up Podman Machine image at"* ]] && continue
    53→  [[ "$clean_line" == *"podman machine start"*              ]] && continue
    54→
    55→  echo "$clean_line" >> "$outfile"
    56→done
    57→
    58→# eof
    59→

input.old_string:
[[ "$clean_line" == "Looking up Podman Machine image at"* ]] && continue
  [[ "$clean_line" == *"podman machine start"*              ]] && continue
input.new_string:
[[ "$clean_line" != "Looking up Podman Machine image at"* ]] || continue
  [[ "$clean_line" != *"podman machine start"*              ]] || continue

content.content: The file /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbw/rbupmis_Scrub.sh has been updated successfully.

content.text: Both edits applied. Lines 52-53 in `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbw/rbupmis_Scrub.sh` now use inverted-test `|| continue` instead of `&& continue`. Alignment of the closing `]]` was preserved.
